author: @WalterBurton
text: Ver cool. 👍

Congratulations on passing your test!!
Well done hiding your "little dent!" Lol!
I won't tell any one,, Okiedokie?! 
(Nice job getting that ding out!! )
Andrea and Pigeons. XxX

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: Seems to have worked, just a repaint job on the scratched bumper from the concrete.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes, it looks good now for sure, If I could do one thing different I would have done it on grass or put down some soft material to prevent scratches. Thanks mate! :)

author: @Charlie-Oooooo
text: Keep up the good we work Luke! 👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you very much mate! :)

author: @PVflying
text: I can see a whole spin off channel emerging here - Luke’s car maintenance learning journey!  There’s so much you can do yourself to service and repair a car if you’re prepared to get stuck in, and so much info available on YouTube and forums, so no need to rely on the old Haynes manual any more!

replies.author: @theaccidentalmechanics
replies.text: exactly what I was thinking, and i don't mind it personally 🙂

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: I THINK THAT "Obi-Wan Kenobi" GAVE YOU THE KEYS TO THE LAND SPEEDER TOO SOON....."LUKE", NEXT TIME YOU POUR WATER, MAKE SURE IT WILL NOT RUN DOWN ON THE GROUND WHERE YOU HAVE TO LAY DOWN ON TO RE INSTALL THE BUMPER. UNLESS YOU ENJOY LAYING IN WATER ON THE GROUND.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: hahahaha yes 100%. I didn't think about it till afterwards when I had to get drenched in a puddle of water LOL. Cheers mate! :)

author: @docink6175
text: congratulations on passing your test and getting your wheels! That certainly worked well. I've had decent luck just heating the outside of the dent with a heat gun or placing a heater nearby and then pouring cold water over the dent..

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you! It has been a long time coming, That is an awesome suggestion, I have one more dent to try and get out lol, I will give this ago. Thanks mate! :)

author: @dexterconnolly3696
text: Nice vid bro. I think if you don't use hot water the paint will definitely crack. I just pulled apart a dji cellphone gimbal. It runs with 3 small hollow shaft brushless 15 pole 3 phase motors. verry interesting.

author: @stevenfaber3896
text: I've also seen actual body shops use a (commercial) handheld pant steamer to achieve the same effect with less water consumption, perhaps. Maybe try this on the front dent?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That's really cool! would definitely save on water consumption. I would try that on the front, but it's on the passenger side quarter panel which is metal. I think I would have to use a dent puller or a very small hammer LOL. Thanks mate! :)

author: @ChopShopGarage
text: For small not creased dents in the metal you can try the heat and sudden cooling (upside down computer cleaning spray) to pop them.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Does this work? Because that would be AWESOME! I have a very small dent on the passenger quarter panel which is made out of metal, I want to give that ago for sure! Thank you for the suggestion mate! :)

author: @YoureNowOnTV
text:
Nice one Luke that was some great work using “The Force” 😁👍
I’m not familiar with this brand of car, for most of the video I thought it said “Giza” as in “Check out this Giza pouring water onto his bumper!” 😆

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahaha LOL! It's actually a seat Ibiza, but rob did suggest changing the "B" to a "G"(Giza) that would be hilarious. Thank you mate! :).

replies.author: @YoureNowOnTV
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey ohhhh I see. Nice car in any case. Congrats on getting your license 😀

author: @wheelhub8922
text: Thanks for showing this! I have the very same model. This car is tough as a boot! I hope you enjoy driving it, my friend. Cheers!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is awesome! It's always nice to encounter another Ibiza owner! I'm absolutely loving the car definitely and made the right decision. Thank you mate! :)

author: @huwkelvinmorgan3575
text: I had just woke up from a power nap and read the description of the video as how to remove dentures from your bumper and could not stop laughing never mind its all down to the reading glasses at my age lol

author: @chrisstevens2
text: Looks like yo forgot to keep some hot water aside for tea after the fix!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: The one draw back LOL! had to wait a full 5 MINS hahaha. Thanks mate! :)

author: @fritanke2318
text: Have to remember this one. Now, if there is a way to mend a hole in the tail light I'm all ears 😂

replies.author: @stevenfaber3896
replies.text: Clear or red transparent tape; dependent upon the section you're replacing; to achieve maximum assistance you can also grab any of the broken pieces and put them on the tape. Works until inspection here in the US. It's also relatively water resistant.

replies.author: @gunna2cool
replies.text: Bit of resin and dye

replies.author: @fritanke2318
replies.text: @stevenfaber3896 Unforrunately inspection is about to arrive. I have used a clear red tape so fare (18 months). Worked so great I forgot about it. Now my laziness is catching up with me 🙄

author: @karlmyers6518
text:
Insurance must be steep. True Gary car that Luke lol 
I'm only jealous

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: hahaha "true Gary car" I love that LOL. Might have to name it Gary now hahaha. Insurance isn't too bad compared to other cars I was looking at. But yes it's still very steep LOL! Thanks mate! :)

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: Well well…..🙂👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @richysee
text: I imagine that doing this would be immensely better on grass so that the concrete doesn't scratch the paintwork.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Oh yes for sure! Definitely less likely to scratch your paintwork lol. Thanks mate! :)

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Would a heat gun / hairdryer work just the same as hot water?

replies.author: @richysee
replies.text: My son does this for a living

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text: A hairdryer if it has plenty of power, a heat gun if it has plenty of control.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I don't know for sure, but I would not use a heat gun because the hot heat could ruin the clear coat of the paint if set too high, not saying it wouldn't work though. As for the hairdryer if it could heat up to some nice temps I could see that working for sure. Although I haven't tried these methods. Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey cool beans. DVD:)

author: @stonestreaker
text: I usually do the Father Ted technique.

replies.author: @onemansjunk01
replies.text: Feck it!!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha LOL! I wouldn't have got this reference until recently. super funny xD

author: @jerrywhidby.
text: The only criticism I would offer is that there was no attempt on camera to push it out before adding hot water.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
I did think about doing that, but I did not want to run the risk of cracking the plastic when it was cold. That's why I used hot water to soften it first.
understandably it is worth a go! Thank you mate! :)

replies.author: @karlmyers6518
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey plastic and paint assuming it's painted lol

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@Jerry Whidby 
That'd probably break the plastic,,,it's why you Need to heat it!!

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
